Petition By Executor To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To The Estate
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Button for and with Janet Melendrez (Executor).

The Court noted that the middle name of Mee Wong is different on every document. Ms. Button stated that she is not aware of any objections, that she filed a declaration yesterday which is in the Court's e-file queue, that the Executor hired a Private Investigator and that he found a Mee Gin Wong with the same date of death, the same date of birth and the same residence. Ms. Button also stated that the death certificate links Ms. Wong to Peter, that her name is listed on the trust, that it also addresses Probate Code 15205 (a) and that a second trustee had recorded the property. The Petition to Establish Estates Claim of Ownership to Property, etc. is under submission pending filing and review of said declaration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Continued To Nov-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Melendrez in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that it is aware the inventory and appraisal was submitted, but it is not imaged and it is unable to see it. Ms. Melendrez stated that it is a final inventory. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is continued to November 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Continued To Nov-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Janet M. Melendrez, Petitioner, appearing in pro per;
Anne Marie Paolini-Mori, Esq. also appeared..

Ms. Paolini-Mori stated that she has not substituted in as attorney of record at this point but she doesn't believe that there should be a probate. The Court stated that a probate has been open, that the time to file an inventory & appraisal has passed, and that the matter had been sent to a probate referee. The Court also stated that the inventory & appraisal form would be helpful. The Court addressed Ms. Melendez stating that she requested to be appointed and that she has a fiduciary obligation to continue administering the estate. Ms. Paolini-Mori stated that Ms. Melendez sent the inventory & appraisal to the probate referee, that the probate referee contacted her (Ms. Paolini-Mori) about the matter, that the property is in a trust which they don't have a copy of, and that at this point they are unable to answer the Court's questions. The Court stated that "bells have gone off", that someone needs to say something, and that if the probate shouldn't be open someone needs to file something. The Court continued the matter to November 13, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the inventory & appraisal will be on file. If the inventory & appraisal is not on file because it is not required/needed then a document addressing why it is not needed should be filed. The filing should address how the administration of the estate will proceed and should indicate that the assets are in a trust.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Continued To Oct-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Melendrez in Propria Persona.

Ms. Melendrez stated that the inventory and appraisal was submitted to the Probate Referee on August 4th and that is has been discovered that the property subject to probate is in a trust. Ms. Melendrez also stated that she has consulted with Attorney Anne Marie Paolini, that she will be retaining Ms. Paolini and that the trust has not been found. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is continued to October 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Appearances: Janet Melendrez in propria persona (Petitioner)

The Court made inquiries regarding Ms. Melendrez's knowledge of probate procedures, whether she knows how to proceed without an attorney, and noted that this estate is not a simple case. Ms. Melendrez stated that she this estate involves one home and three beneficiaries, that she has consulted with an attorney, she hasn't had problems so far, she will prepare Letters, she knows how to do an inventory, and she can consult with an attorney with any questions. The Court granted The Amended Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order) with Limited Authority; court approval needed for sale of real property. Status on filing of Inventory and Appraisal is set for September 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of Petition for Final Distribution is set for November 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
